field_verdicts.notes: Confirmed directly against Rev. Proc. 2025-32, section 4.01, Table 1, as exposed in the official IRS Internal Revenue Bulletin text and PDF mirrors. Current values match exactly.
Confirmed directly against Rev. Proc. 2025-32, section 4.01, Table 1, in the official IRS materials. Current values match exactly.
Confirmed directly against Rev. Proc. 2025-32, section 4.01, Table 3, in the official IRS materials. Current values match exactly.

Confirmed directly against Rev. Proc. 2025-32, section 4.01, Table 4, in the official IRS materials. The official 24% upper threshold is $201,750, matching current_value.json and disagreeing with src_tf_1.
Confirmed directly against Rev. Proc. 2025-32, section 4.01, Table 1, which explicitly covers married individuals filing joint returns and surviving spouses. Current values match exactly.

notes: All five filing-status schedules in current_value.json match the official IRS 2026 tax rate tables in Rev. Proc. 2025-32. I independently inspected the official IRS PDF citation and an official IRS Internal Revenue Bulletin HTML mirror for the same revenue procedure, which resolves the original run's PDF-parsing limitation. Source-level caveat: src_tf_1 appears to misstate the head-of-household 24% upper breakpoint as $201,775 instead of the official $201,750. src_doeren_1 matches the IRS tables but does not count toward status because www.doeren.com is outside the allowed secondary-host policy.

source_verdicts.reason: Accepted as the complete authoritative source. Rev. Proc. 2025-32 section 4.01 Tables 1 through 5 directly provide the 2026 bracket schedules for all filing statuses, and the values match current_value.json exactly.

Accepted as an official IRS summary that corroborates the single and married-filing-jointly schedules, but it is incomplete for full-field verification because it does not publish the complete set of filing-status tables.
Rejected for this dataset because the Tax Foundation table appears to state the head-of-household 24% upper threshold as $201,775, while the official IRS revenue procedure shows $201,750.
Accepted as factually consistent with the IRS tables, but it does not count toward status because www.doeren.com is not on the allowed secondary-host list in source_policy.json.
